hooks.command: python -c "import sys, platform; print(f'[SYSTEM CONTEXT] OS: {sys.platform} | Platform: {platform.system()} {platform.release()} | Shell note: use PowerShell-compatible commands on win32, bash on linux')"

MESSAGE=$(cat | jq -r '.message // empty'); echo "$MESSAGE" | grep -qiE 'archilume.app|archilume_app|reflex|rx\.|component|state|styling|frontend|ui' && echo 'REFLEX DOCS REMINDER: This prompt may involve Reflex UI work. Before planning or implementing, search .claude/skills/reflex-docs/reference/ for relevant patterns. Read llms.txt for the overview, then grep components/core dirs for specifics. Use WebFetch on reflex.dev if local docs are insufficient. Do not invent workarounds when docs exist.' || true

MESSAGE=$(cat | jq -r '.message // empty'); echo "$MESSAGE" | grep -qiE 'archilume.app|archilume_app|viewport|editor_state|overlay|floor.?plan|df_analysis|image_loader' && echo 'ARCHILUME_APP EXPLORATION HOOK: This prompt focuses on archilume_app. Before attempting any fix or implementation, launch an Explore agent to trace the relevant data flow through the archilume_app codebase (Python state → Reflex event handlers → frontend rendering). Report back the full chain with file:line references before making edits. This preserves main context and prevents wrong-approach spirals.' || true
FILE=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty') && [ -n "$FILE" ] && [[ "$FILE" == *.py ]] && python -m py_compile "$FILE" 2>&1 || true

FILE=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty') && [ -n "$FILE" ] && [[ "$FILE" == *archilume_app* ]] && cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/archilume/apps/archilume_app && python -c "import archilume_app.archilume_app" 2>&1 || true
